title: Mild Smoky Filipino Haricot Beans Sisig
description: A yeasty, crumbly Filipino dish featuring haricot beans, flambe, in a cast-iron skillet, perfect for a weeknight meal.
instructions: Heat the oil in a cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic and sauté until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the anchovy paste and patis, stir until the mixture is well combined, about 1 minute.
Add the vinegar and stir to deglaze the pan, about 1 minute.
Add the coconut milk and stir until the mixture is smooth, about 2 minutes.
Add the haricot beans, Thai chili peppers, salt, and black pepper, stir well to combine.
Flambé the mixture with a lighter or matches until the liquid has mostly evaporated and the mixture is slightly smoky.
Mix in the flour and cook for 1 minute.
Remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the unsalted butter until melted and well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a serving platter or individual plates, garnish with chopped Thai chili peppers if desired, and serve hot.
